text.title: enum-constraints-closed-vocabularies Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules for enum constraints on finite-value fields.

rule.statement: Every classification or routing field with a finite value set MUST be declared as `Literal[...]` (Python) or `enum` (JSON Schema), NOT a free-form string with values listed in the description.
rule.rationale: Strict-mode constrained decoders mask all non-enum tokens to probability zero, eliminating hallucinated labels. Free-string fields keep the full vocabulary live; smaller models occasionally emit synonyms or novel labels that break downstream switch/case code.

rule.statement: Enum-constrained fields MUST be served under strict mode OR a constrained decoder backend (XGrammar, Outlines, vLLM guided generation); JSON-mode-only fallback paths are advisory and require post-validation.
rule.rationale: Without sampling-time masking, enums are documentation only. Mini/nano models hallucinate enum values under JSON-mode fallback; the schema is shown but never enforced.

rule.statement: For semi-open sets, model the field as a discriminated union: a `Literal[...]` branch for known values plus a typed "novel" branch carrying the free-text candidate; NEVER use `Literal[...] | str` directly.
rule.rationale: A discriminated union forces the decoder to commit to "known" or "novel" first, then constrains the rest. Plain `Literal | str` collapses to `str` in strict mode and loses the guarantee.

rule.statement: When strict mode is not available, the orchestrator MUST validate the parsed enum value after the call and either retry-with-feedback ("you returned X; valid values are A/B/C") OR map to the nearest enum via a deterministic table.
rule.rationale: Production systems on JSON-mode-only fallback must reconcile drift; silent drift means downstream switch/case crashes.
